text: Profile raises silently change which machinery gates fire: raising a task routine->high activates the H9 spec-ambiguity gate (BLOCKING_AMBIGUITY_PROFILES=high,critical) on its CARRIED-OVER test-author handoff — advisory ambiguities become blockers that park before the implementer. After any profile raise on a task with an existing handoff, re-run ambiguity_blockers and adjudicate before spawning.

Runtime gotcha (python 3.14 / bcrypt 5.0.0 / passlib 1.7.4): passlib bcrypt is BROKEN here (passlib reads bcrypt.__about__.__version__, removed in bcrypt 5.0, then raises a spurious 72-byte error). Despite the spec wording 'bcrypt via passlib', use the `bcrypt` library DIRECTLY: hash_password = bcrypt.hashpw(pw.encode('utf-8')[:72], bcrypt.gensalt()).decode(); verify_password = bcrypt.checkpw(pw.encode('utf-8')[:72], hash.encode()). Yields $2b$ hashes satisfying 'password_hash starts with $2'.

Runtime gotcha: FastAPI/Starlette Jinja2Templates.TemplateResponse requires the (request, name, context) signature — call templates.TemplateResponse(request, 'x.html', {...}) with request FIRST, not the deprecated (name, {'request': request}). app.state.templates is already a Jinja2Templates instance set by create_app().

GL2 ships no route taking an object id, so the CRITICAL cross-tenant-404 invariant is enforced at the HELPER level: app.security.get_or_404(db, Model, id, user) must raise fastapi.HTTPException(status_code=404) for BOTH a nonexistent id AND an id owned by another company (uniform; never 403, never a redirect); company_query(db, Model, user) filters by user.company_id. These helpers MUST be importable from app.security.

Session payload 'user_id ONLY' coexists with storing csrf_token in the session — the enforceable invariant is 'no role/company/tenant data smuggled into the session', NOT literally one key. A live session legitimately holds both user_id and csrf_token.

Preserve the scaffold DB convention: the `app` test fixture AND create_app() both set app.state.engine and app.state.session_factory and override get_db via app.dependency_overrides[get_db]. Keep create_app() setting these and keep get_db as the request DB dependency so the in-memory test DB stays wired.
